root_cause_candidates.reasoning: All sources point to frontend as the symptom. There is no log evidence of upstream faults, and topology shows multiple downstream dependencies without distinct propagation edges. TCB-RCA also concludes with frontend as the traversal node, but with zero anomaly evidence. Since there is no unobserved upstream service indicated by logs (no B with upstream reference), we cannot elevate any deeper service as root. The strongest statement is that a root cause remains unobserved in available data; frontend is the only node implicated in the evidence we have, but it is not a confirmed upstream/root cause.

result.synthesis_reasoning: There is complete disagreement about a concrete root cause from the three sources: log evidence shows no anomalies, topology identifies the frontend as the symptom with no confirmed upstream edge, and the TCB-RCA analysis yields the symptom node with zero anomalies. Because there is no observed upstream failure to attribute to a deeper service and no unobserved root-cause indication (e.g., a referenced upstream with no logs), we cannot reliably identify a deeper root cause. The most defensible position is that no credible root cause is detectable with the given data; if pushed to name a candidate, frontend remains the only node implicated by the evidence set, but this is acknowledged as weak and not a true root cause.
